example_sentence: Todo medio tiene algún sesgo; lo importante es identificarlo.
example_translation: Every outlet has some bias; what matters is identifying it.

example_sentence: El encuadre de la noticia determina cómo la percibe el público.
example_translation: The framing of the news story determines how the public perceives it.

translation: editorial line / political stance of an outlet
example_sentence: Antes de citar una fuente, conviene conocer su línea editorial.
example_translation: Before citing a source, it is worth knowing its editorial line.

example_sentence: El acusado alega que no estaba presente en el momento de los hechos.
example_translation: The accused alleges that he was not present at the time of the events.

example_sentence: La empresa admitió que los datos de usuarios habían sido filtrados.
example_translation: The company admitted that user data had been leaked.

example_sentence: La desinformación en redes sociales complica el debate público.
example_translation: Disinformation on social media complicates public debate.

example_sentence: El reportaje de investigación expuso la red de corrupción en el municipio.
example_translation: The investigative report exposed the corruption network in the municipality.

example_sentence: Un buen periodista siempre verifica la información con más de una fuente.
example_translation: A good journalist always verifies information with more than one source.

example_sentence: El secretario sostiene que las cifras oficiales son correctas.
example_translation: The secretary maintains that the official figures are correct.

example_sentence: El titular sensacionalista no refleja con precisión el contenido del artículo.
example_translation: The sensationalist headline does not accurately reflect the article's content.

translation: in the judgment of / in the view of
example_sentence: A juicio de varios analistas, la política económica es insostenible.
example_translation: In the view of several analysts, the economic policy is unsustainable.

translation: to acknowledge / to recognize (implies previous denial)
example_sentence: El gobierno reconoció que los datos habían sido manipulados.
example_translation: The government acknowledged that the data had been manipulated.
